questionText: A particle has position $x = 4t^{2} - t$ and $y = t^{3} + 2t$ metres at time $t$ seconds. Find the horizontal and vertical components of its velocity, $\dot{x}$ and $\dot{y}$, when $t = 1$.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: The horizontal velocity $\dot{x}$ is the derivative of $x = 4t^{2} - t$, showing how fast the particle moves sideways.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: The vertical velocity $\dot{y}$ is the derivative of $y = t^{3} + 2t$, showing how fast the height changes.

steps.description: Substitute t = 1 into the horizontal velocity
steps.explanation: Putting $t = 1$ into $\dot{x}$ gives the sideways speed at that particular moment.
Working out the arithmetic gives the horizontal component of the velocity.

steps.description: Substitute t = 1 into the vertical velocity
steps.explanation: Putting $t = 1$ into $\dot{y}$ gives the vertical speed at the same moment.
Working out the arithmetic gives the vertical component of the velocity.
So at $t = 1$ second the particle moves at $7$ m/s horizontally and $5$ m/s vertically.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: When $t = 1$ s the velocity components are $\dot{x} = 7$ m/s and $\dot{y} = 5$ m/s.
